criteria.criterion: In `.github/workflows/test.yml`, the `pre-commit run --all-files` step's `SKIP` env includes `lint` and `typecheck` (as well as `no-commit-to-branch`).
In `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml`, the mirrored `pre-commit run --all-files` step applies the identical `SKIP` set.
The `lint` and `typecheck` hooks remain PRESENT and unchanged in `.pre-commit-config.yaml` (a local `git commit` still runs them).
`make lint` and `make typecheck` remain as their own named gating steps in both workflows (each linter still runs exactly once in CI).
A parity-test assertion in `tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py` fails the build if the CI pre-commit step stops skipping `lint`/`typecheck` (regression guard). Proven RED→GREEN.

citation.description: Untagged criterion; inherently operational but the implementation is fully correct — both direct steps remain in the workflows, both hooks remain in .pre-commit-config.yaml, and no code changes affect lint/typecheck behavior. No contrary evidence found in the repo.

criteria.criterion: `make lint` and `make typecheck` are green locally.
